title: Event-Driven Employee Notifications for Benefits Lifecycle Events
description: The system shall trigger employee notifications for defined benefits lifecycle events. Each event type shall have a configured notification rule specifying recipient(s), channel(s), template, and delivery timing.

title: Email and In-App Notification Channels
description: The system shall support Email and In-App notification channels. Email is the primary channel for benefits communication. In-App notifications provide real-time alerts within the employee portal. PDF document generation is the third MVP channel for formal benefits events.

title: Configurable Notification Templates
description: The system shall support configurable notification templates managed by Benefits Administrators. Each template is associated with an event type, channel, subject, body, optional attachments, language, and version. Templates must be approved before activation.

title: Dynamic Merge Fields in Templates
description: Notification templates shall support dynamic merge fields for employee, employer, plan, coverage, event, and portal URL data. The system shall substitute merge fields at send time.

title: Life Event Notification Sequence
description: The system shall send notifications at each stage of the life event lifecycle: submitted, document required, document received, approved/rejected, coverage updated, coverage effective. Each stage corresponds to a distinct notification event.

notificationSequence: Life Event Received — Upload marriage certificate by deadline
Life Event Approved — Spouse now eligible
Coverage Confirmed — Spouse added to Medical PPO-500 and Dental effective July 1

title: Coverage Change Notification
description: When a life event results in a benefits coverage change, the system shall notify the employee of the specific change: plans affected, effective date, new coverage tier, and updated premium deduction.

title: Open Enrollment Open and Closing Soon Notifications
description: The system shall send notifications to eligible employees when Open Enrollment opens and before it closes (configurable advance notice, default: 3 days). The closing notification shall include remaining days and a direct enrollment link.

title: Enrollment Submission Confirmation
description: The system shall send a confirmation notification when an employee successfully submits an enrollment election. The confirmation shall include a summary of elected plans, coverage tiers, effective date, and employee premium.

title: Notification Delivery Status Tracking
description: The system shall track notification delivery lifecycle: CREATED → QUEUED → SENT → DELIVERED → OPENED → ACKNOWLEDGED. Failed notifications shall transition to FAILED and support configurable retry.

title: Retry Handling for Failed Notifications
description: The system shall support configurable retry logic for failed notifications. After the maximum retry count is reached, the notification status shall be set to FAILED_PERMANENTLY and an administrator alert shall be generated.

title: Benefits Confirmation Document Generation
description: The system shall generate a benefits confirmation PDF document for applicable coverage change events (enrollment confirmation, life event coverage change, COBRA notice, eligibility determination). The document shall be stored and linked to the notification.

title: Employee Document Center
description: The system shall maintain a document center in the employee portal where generated benefits documents are stored. Employees shall be able to view, download, and acknowledge documents. Documents shall be filterable by year, event type, and plan.

title: Notification History and Audit Records
description: The system shall maintain an audit record for every notification: event trigger, template selected, channel, recipient, sent timestamp, delivery status, document ID (if generated), and acknowledgment status. Notification audit integrates with the platform-wide audit trail (REQ-AUD-001).

title: Mandatory vs. Optional Notification Distinction
description: The system shall distinguish MANDATORY communications (legally or plan-required notices) from OPTIONAL notifications (enrollment reminders, plan update notices). Mandatory communications shall be delivered regardless of employee notification preferences.

title: Mandatory Communications Cannot Be Suppressed
description: Employees shall not be able to disable mandatory legally or plan-required notifications through their communication preferences. The UI shall display mandatory communications as non-configurable.

title: Administrator Alerts for Operational Exceptions
description: The system shall send administrator-directed alerts for critical carrier, payroll, and reconciliation exceptions: carrier transaction failure, payroll mismatch, integration monitoring failure, and reconciliation exception. Administrator alerts are separate from employee-facing communications.

title: Configurable Employee Notification Preferences
description: Employees shall be able to configure preferences for optional notifications: channel preference (email, in-app, SMS), delivery frequency, and document delivery method (electronic vs. paper). Mandatory communications are exempt from preference-based suppression.
